title: Align joystick video scrub speed to the 15-second transport step
summary: 0.6.43 fixes the CUA joystick video scrub seam so it moves at the same 15-second base rate as the shared skip transport instead of using the older duration-scaled normalized scrub speed.
changes: The CUA joystick video scrub path now derives its normalized movement from the shared 15-second transport step.
Long videos should no longer feel like they are scrubbing by roughly 90 seconds per second just because the input seam was using a normalized rate.
reasoning: 0.6.42 changed the shared skip seam to 15 seconds, but the CUA joystick scrub path still had its own older normalized-rate constant, so the operator was feeling two different transport policies at once.
The safest fix is to align the CUA scrub seam to the shared transport step instead of widening the transport model again.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Joystick video scrub should now feel like the intended 15-second movement instead of the older much-faster rate on long videos.
knownIssues: This slice does not add the stateful A-B checkbox yet.
This slice does not yet make image playlists use the slider as an index transport.
